description: Manual cross-links between mental models. These create the 'lattice' connections across domains.

crosslinks.reason: Kelly uses Bayesian probability estimation for optimal betting
Both deal with decision-making under uncertainty
Kelly requires the probability distribution of outcomes; fat tails break the formula
Kelly is optimal strategy for a one-player game against nature
Compound interest drives debt accumulation in economic cycles
Compound interest is the textbook recursive formula: f(n) = f(n-1) * (1+r)
Entropy measures uncertainty in probability distributions
Information theory and Bayesian inference share mathematical foundations
Both explain human behavior and decision-making
Games are incentive structures; incentives determine the strategies players choose
Debt cycles are driven by incentive structures like cheap credit and moral hazard
Prospect theory documents how humans violate Bayesian updating via base rate neglect
Loss aversion and overweighting of tail events connect to fat-tailed distributions
Strategic reasoning is recursive: I think that you think that I think...
